loanAssumptions.rateMethod: Uses SBA variable-rate ceilings by loan size as a conservative estimate.

candidates.name: CPA Practice - Hernando County Florida - $365K Revenue
candidates.notes: Tax, bookkeeping, and payroll mix. Listing notes a solid bookkeeper expected to continue.

underwriting_details.why_it_fits: Recurring tax, bookkeeping, and payroll revenue with long operating history and an apparent transition plan around a retained bookkeeper.
revenue_and_cash_flow_view: Initial take: strong fit on recurring service lines and cash flow level; price is reasonable relative to cash flow.
underwriting_details.key_risks: Seasonality, client concentration, and how much production still sits directly with the owner need validation.
financing_view: Preliminary view: likely SBA-serviceable if client retention and transferability hold up.
missing_facts: Is the seller retiring now, or just testing the market?
How many clients are payroll/monthly bookkeeping versus annual tax-only?
What percentage of revenue is tied to the top ten accounts?
What staff besides the bookkeeper support delivery?
Is there any seller carry available?

candidates.name: High Margin 24-Year Managed IT Services Provider
candidates.notes: 24-year operating history and solid cash flow. Recurring managed services profile.

underwriting_details.why_it_fits: Long operating history, strong disclosed cash flow, and recurring managed services make this one of the better listing-backed technical service targets.
revenue_and_cash_flow_view: Initial take: cash flow is attractive, but the asking price requires diligence on contract durability and owner dependence.
underwriting_details.key_risks: Need clarity on whether this is owner-heavy despite the strong margins, and whether the customer base is concentrated.
financing_view: Preliminary view: good business quality on paper, but SBA leverage may be tight at this price without seller carry.
missing_facts: Owner role in sales and technical delivery

candidates.name: Fairfax County Full Service CPA & Bookkeeping
candidates.notes: Founded in 2008. Full-service CPA firm with accounting, financial statements, tax planning, and bookkeeping.

underwriting_details.why_it_fits: Full-service CPA and bookkeeping mix with meaningful cash flow, long history, and a service bundle that tends to support recurring relationships.
revenue_and_cash_flow_view: Initial take: attractive economics, but valuation is richer than the smaller practice listings.
underwriting_details.key_risks: Need to validate staffing depth, client concentration, and whether the asking price still makes sense after transition and retention risk.
financing_view: Preliminary view: likely financeable, but leverage will be heavier and diligence must support the higher price.
missing_facts: Mix of bookkeeping, tax, and higher-value advisory work
Seller transition terms and willingness to carry paper

candidates.name: Established Managed IT Services Provider with Recurring Revenue
candidates.notes: Recurring managed services provider with 3 contractors and documented operations.

underwriting_details.why_it_fits: Managed IT with explicit recurring revenue is a strong structural fit and this listing includes some operator depth via contractors.
revenue_and_cash_flow_view: Initial take: attractive business model, but price is high relative to current cash flow.
underwriting_details.key_risks: Valuation appears full, contractor reliance needs validation, and contract stickiness has to be proven.
financing_view: Preliminary view: may be financeable, but likely stretched on SBA debt service unless there is sizable seller carry or stronger normalized cash flow.
missing_facts: What percentage of revenue is on contract versus project work?
Are the contractors stable and transferable?
What is churn over the last 24 months?
Is there upside from add-on services not reflected in current cash flow?
Will the seller provide transition support or seller paper?

candidates.name: Consulting, Bookkeeping and Accounting Firm
candidates.notes: Bookkeeping and accounting listing with disclosed cash flow and established client base.

underwriting_details.why_it_fits: Recurring bookkeeping and accounting work is structurally attractive, and the disclosed cash flow falls inside the target buy box.
revenue_and_cash_flow_view: Initial take: price-to-cash-flow looks workable, but team depth and transferability need proof.
underwriting_details.key_risks: The main risk is whether this is really a team-backed client service firm versus an owner-heavy solo practice.
financing_view: Preliminary view: financing looks plausible, but people/process depth is the gating diligence item.
missing_facts: Who currently services the clients besides the owner?
What portion of revenue is recurring monthly versus advisory/project based?
What software stack and workflow documentation exist?

candidates.name: COCoPropertyManagement
candidates.notes: SMB.co listing surfaced from Terrell Hills source page. Revenue/cash flow/valuation are present, but operating history and team depth still need validation.

underwriting_details.why_it_fits: Property management is attractive when doors under management and ops staff are real because revenue can be recurring and sticky.
revenue_and_cash_flow_view: Initial take: economics fit the buy box, but the SMB listing detail is thin and needs validation before moving higher.
underwriting_details.key_risks: The listing appears on an SMB city/source page with limited detail, so team depth, operating history, and actual portfolio quality are all unverified.
financing_view: Preliminary view: SBA payments should be manageable on paper, but confidence is lower until the business is validated directly.

candidates.name: GPRES Property Management
candidates.notes: SMB.co listing surfaced from Oakmont source page. Revenue/cash flow/valuation are present, but operating history and team depth still need validation.

underwriting_details.why_it_fits: Property management can fit the recurring-revenue buy box if the managed portfolio and operating system are legitimate and transferable.
revenue_and_cash_flow_view: Initial take: SMB snippet shows workable revenue, cash flow, and valuation, but detail depth is materially lower than broker listings.
underwriting_details.key_risks: Listing detail is sparse, so portfolio size, staffing, and actual transferability are unclear.
financing_view: Preliminary view: financeable on paper, but diligence confidence is lower until source details are expanded.
